row1.revenue: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) maintains its position as the leader in revenue, with a significant gap of $3.7B USD between itself and Walmart Inc. (WMT), the second-ranked company.
row1.net_income: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with a net income of $132.2B, a significant gap of over $30B from NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) in second place. This substantial lead underscores GOOGL's dominant financial performance in the latest fiscal year.
row1.total_assets: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) maintains its position as the largest company by Total Assets, with a significant lead of $1T USD over the second-largest company, Bank of America Corporation (BAC). The gap between JPM and BAC is substantial, highlighting the dominance of JPM in terms of asset size.
row1.total_equity: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads with $415.3B in Total Equity, outpacing Amazon.com (AMZN) by $4.2B. The top two companies hold over 40% of the total equity among the listed companies.
row1.total_liabilities: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $4.1T in Total Liabilities, more than 10 times the liabilities of Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN), the next closest competitor.
row1.cash_and_equivalents: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the top companies with $343.3B in Cash And Equivalents, a significant gap of over $100B from the second-place Bank of America Corporation (BAC). This substantial cash reserve highlights JPM's financial stability and flexibility.
row1.gross_profit: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a Gross Profit of $360.5B USD, significantly outpacing the next closest competitor, Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL), by $120.2B USD.
row1.operating_income_ebit: Apple Inc. (AAPL) leads the pack with a staggering $133.1B Operating Income Ebit, a significant margin above NVIDIA Corporation's (NVDA) $130.4B. The top 3 companies (AAPL, NVDA, and GOOGL) account for over 60% of the combined Operating Income Ebit of the top 5.

row2_sector.revenue: Consumer Cyclical and Technology sectors dominate the revenue landscape, accounting for 43.1% of the total revenue, with Consumer Cyclical leading at 22.0% and Technology following closely at 21.1%.
row2_sector.total_assets: Financial Services sector dominates the sector distribution by Total Assets with 64.1% market share, significantly outpacing the next closest sector, Technology, with 9.7%. This concentration suggests a high reliance on the Financial Services sector within the overall market.
row2_sector.net_income: Technology sector dominates the net income landscape with 39.2% share, nearly 2x the combined share of Communication Services and Financial Services. This highlights the significant profitability of the tech industry.

row2_bubble: NVDA's 55.6% net margin significantly outpaces UNH's 2.7%, highlighting NVDA's superior profitability efficiency. This stark contrast underscores the vastly different business models and operational efficiencies between the two companies.
row3_heatmap: Morgan Stanley's (MA) 193.5% return on equity is significantly higher than its peers, but its leverage is relatively moderate at 2.44x. In contrast, JPMorgan Chase's (JPM) high leverage of 11.21x comes with a lower ROE of 12.1%, highlighting a trade-off between returns and risk.
row4_market_trends: GOOGL's 12-month cumulative return of +108.2% stands out as the best performer among high-revenue companies, while ADBE's -42.2% return marks the worst performance in the same group. This significant disparity highlights the varying market fortunes of these top-revenue businesses. GOOGL's exceptional growth may be worth closer examination.
